titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Kennedy Papers
titlestmt.author: by Tina Evans and Rosemary K. J. Davis

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain arrangement present at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Paul Kennedy
bioghist.p: Paul M. Kennedy (1945-) is the J. Richardson Dilworth Professor of History, Director of International Security Studies at Yale University. He is most well known for his writings, editorial work, and teaching related to global economics, politics, and strategy.

Born in England, Kennedy received a BA from Newcastle University and a DPhil from the University of Oxford. He holds multiple honorary degrees, has held many respected positions and fellowships, and is author, contributor for, or editor of over twenty volumes including his best-known work, The Rise and Fall of the Great Powers (1987).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Kennedy Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, printed materials, teaching materials, and other papers created by, or related to the life and work of, Paul Kennedy.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Kennedy Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.
